rulings.Case Title: DAVID ABRAMOVITZ ET AL VS. FARHAT BAJJALIYA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaitiff David Abramovitzs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Farhat Bajjalyiyas Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set Five, Number 268-282, 285-291, 295-297 And 300; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Farhat Bajjaliya And His Attorney Of Record, Todd Angstadt, In The Amount Of $744.95
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 15, 2019, Line 5, 1) PLAINTIFF DAVID ABRAMOVITZ's Motion To Compel Defendant Farhat Bajjalyiyas Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set Five, Number 268-282, 285-291, 295-297 And 300; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Farhat Bajjaliya And His Attorney Of Record, Todd Angstadt, In The Amount Of $744.95 Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. All objections are overruled. Defendant to serve a verified response to Interrogatories No. Number 268-282, 285-291, 295-297 And 300 no later than December 2, 2019. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Defendant to serve a check no later than December 2, 2019 made payable to the Client Trust Fund of The Law Offices of Mary Catherine Wiederhold in the amount of $744.95. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA BERRY ET AL VS. SUTTER VALLEY HOSPITALS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals, Inc.'S Motion To Enter Judgment Of Dismissal Per Ccp 664.6 And 998
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 15, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT SUTTER VALLEY HOSPITALS, INC.'S Motion To Enter Judgment Of Dismissal Per Ccp 664.6 And 998 Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals, Inc.'s "motion to enter judgment of dismissal per CCP §664.6 and §998" is denied. While defendant's desire for efficiency is noted, it concedes that plaintiff Karizma Berry did not accept its §998 offer within the 30-day statutory period. Thus, "no valid acceptance was effected before the offer was withdrawn by operation of law." (Drouin v. Fleetwood Enterprises (1985) 163 Cal.App.3d 486, 492.) Defendant cites no authority for "waiving" the §998 statutory period and I know of none. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JONJO DIASE VS. J.P. MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 15, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANTD CARLOS INFANTE, JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendants JP Morgan Chase Bank N.A. and Carlos Infante demur to all five causes of action in plaintiff Jonjo Diase's complaint; the demurrers are all overruled: 1: An Unruh Act (Civ. Code §51) claim is adequately pled; plaintiff alleges he was denied access by his bank to money on the basis of his race. 2: An intentional infliction of emotional distress claim is adequately pled; swearing at a customer, ordering him to "get the fuck out" of a bank and having an armed policeman eject him is outrageous conduct that could cause severe emotional distress. 3: A Bane Act (Civ. Code §52.1) claim is adequately pled; ordering a customer to "get the fuck out" of a bank and having an armed policeman eject him qualifies as a threat of violence. 4: A bank depositor is not precluded from suing his bank for conversion. (Fong v. East West Bank (2018) 19 Cal.App.5th 224, 231-33.) Morse v. Crocker National Bank (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 228, 232 is not to the contrary; it provides that a bank is obligated to pay a depositor on demand. 5: Depriving a bank depositor of access to his funds could qualify as economic injury under Business & Professions Code §§17200 and 17500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Uber is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
